metrics.narrative: The Customer_Business_Problem__c field lays out a credible, specific pain: Jackson Lewis has no legitimate procurement function today, spend and contracts live siloed across departments on an ad-hoc basis, there is no centralized contract repository, no way to track expirations, and redlining is described as 'a back and forth game.' Business_Objectives__c and Value_to_Customer__c name centralized procurement, faster contract cycles, and a foundation for broader S2P transformation as the goals. None of this has yet been quantified in dollars, hours, or risk exposure by the buyer directly in a recorded call (both Avoma sessions are unread — one not_recorded, one has no transcript/notes), so it calibrates as a real but unquantified business case.

champion.narrative: No champion is identified. Melissa Geddie is the sole contact and is logged as an Evaluator, not an advocate. A material risk sits in Why_not_Zycus__c: 'Melissa has spoken with Zycus a number of times in her work history, but has never chosen us as VOC in any projects' — meaning the one contact on this deal has a personal track record of engaging Zycus repeatedly across her career without ever selecting it. She did react positively to Merlin Intake in Teams during early conversations, but that enthusiasm has not been followed by any committee formation, discovery booking, or renewed contact in the 108 days since the 23 Mar 2026 sync.

competition.narrative: Coupa, GEP Worldwide, Ivalua, and JAGGAER are logged as competitors, and the 23 Mar 2026 note confirms Melissa is 'already in talks with vendors.' How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c indicates there is no existing centralized system today (likely manual/fragmented tools), so this reads as a greenfield, multi-vendor evaluation rather than an incumbent displacement. No call evidence exists on where any competitor currently stands relative to Zycus, since neither Avoma session has a usable transcript or notes.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is well articulated and specific: Jackson Lewis has no procurement function today (spend managed ad hoc, department by department), no centralized contract repository, no way to track contract expirations, and redlining is manual and inconsistent. Compelling_Event__c ties this directly to Melissa Geddie's own hiring as the firm's first true procurement professional, who is now evaluating S2P systems starting with CLM as an entry point. This is a real, buyer-specific compelling event, not boilerplate.

paper_process.narrative: No paper process exists yet — the deal is at Qualified, pre-discovery, pre-demo. Current_Contract_Expiration__c is blank, consistent with Jackson Lewis having no existing centralized procurement/CLM system to migrate off of.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer has been identified or contacted on this opportunity. Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c and Decision_Maker_Identified__c are both false, X10a_Sponsor__c and Who_will_approve_budget__c are blank, and the only opportunity contact role on record is Melissa Geddie as Evaluator. The account record lists a CFO (Andrew Charwat) and a CTO (Clifford Forrester), but neither has appeared in any call, task, or email tied to this opportunity's scope — they are unengaged account-level contacts, not confirmed stakeholders on this deal. For a $90k, four-product purchase, budget sign-off almost certainly requires someone at this level, and there is currently no path to them.

decision_process.narrative: What_is_the_decision_process__c is blank. The only process signal is Melissa's stated intent on 23 Mar 2026 to form an evaluation committee and that she is 'already in talks with vendors' — this describes an intended process, not a confirmed one, and it has not been reconfirmed or advanced in the 108 days since.

decision_criteria.narrative: Business_Requirements__c is blank and no evaluation criteria have been documented or discussed on any recorded call. The 23 Mar 2026 note references Melissa 'putting together a committee to evaluate solutions for CLM + eInvoicing/AP Automation' but does not name the criteria that committee will use.

flags: RE-ENGAGE the relevant stakeholder: 95 days dark with 62 days to close. The promised disco/demo from 23 Mar never happened and the buyer has not responded. The single highest-leverage move is for the deal owner to directly re-engage Melissa (email + LinkedIn + phone) this week to confirm if the evaluation is still active. If no reply within 7 business days, escalate to the deal owner's manager for an executive-to-executive check-in with a senior Jackson Lewis stakeholder (CFO or CIO from account contact list). If still no engagement, disqualify by end of July and stop investing.

MULTI-THREAD IMMEDIATELY UPON RE-ENGAGEMENT: Single-threaded to Melissa (Sr. Manager, Procurement) with no visibility into the evaluation committee, EB, DM, or cross-functional stakeholders (finance, legal, IT) who must approve a CLM + AP automation decision. The 23 Mar next step promised committee formation but we never met them. Upon re-engagement, demand introductions to at least three committee members, map who controls budget (likely the CFO or finance executive), and identify a champion with power and access. A procurement-only thread in a $90k, multi-product, AI-enabled deal is a loss waiting to happen.

RUN COMPETITIVE DISCOVERY BEFORE DEMO: Four competitors (Coupa, GEP, iValua, JAGGAER) are in play but we have zero intelligence on who has demoed, how the buyer ranks them, or what criteria they are using. Positioning Zycus's differentiation (Merlin AI, integrated CLM + Source-to-Pay) requires knowing the gaps the other vendors leave open. Upon re-engagement, before scheduling a demo, run a 30-minute discovery call to surface: (a) which vendors have already presented, (b) how the committee scored them and on what criteria, (c) what they liked and what fell short, and (d) whether budget and timeline are still live. Then tailor the demo to the gaps Zycus uniquely fills.

The buyer's own committed next step (form a committee, schedule discovery and a demo) went unmet for 108 days, no economic buyer has ever been contacted, and the one contact has a personal history of never selecting Zycus. A rep re-engagement email went out on 1 July 2026 (8 days ago) with no reply yet.

deal_risk.text: With 49 days to the 27 Aug 2026 close date and no discovery, demo, RFP response, EB access, or contracting motion started, the recorded close date is not mathematically achievable.

Only one buyer contact role exists on the opportunity (Melissa Geddie, Evaluator) — a single point of failure with no visibility into who else at Jackson Lewis is involved in the CLM/procurement evaluation.

win_position.text: Stage is Qualified with zero discovery or demo completed against a $90k, four-product ask (iContract, iSource, eProcurement, Merlin) — per Qualified_Submission_Date__c the deal has sat here since 2026-03-23 with no forward movement.

No economic buyer or decision maker has been identified or contacted (Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c=false, Decision_Maker_Identified__c=false); the account's CFO and CTO are unengaged on this opportunity.

The sole buyer contact, Melissa Geddie, has a documented history (Why_not_Zycus__c) of engaging Zycus repeatedly across her career without ever choosing it — a real champion-credibility risk, not just a coverage gap.

Real, specific pain exists and is buyer-linked: Jackson Lewis has no procurement function today and Melissa was hired specifically to build one, with CLM as her stated entry point (Compelling_Event__c, 2026-01-15 intro).

deal_momentum.text: No buyer-side engagement since the 23 Mar 2026 sync (108 days as of 9 Jul 2026); the committed committee-formation and discovery/demo scheduling never occurred.
The only recent activity is a rep-initiated probing email on 1 Jul 2026 (8 days ago) with no buyer reply yet — this is outreach, not engagement.

Melissa did engage twice previously: an incoming email reply on 15 Jan 2026 and full attendance at the 23 Mar 2026 sync (18 minutes, per Avoma), so the relationship is not cold from a first-contact standpoint.

customer_commitment.text: Melissa committed on 23 Mar 2026 to build an evaluation committee and schedule discovery + demo; 108 days later, neither has materialized and there has been no reconfirmation.

hard.ais_why:
• Jackson Lewis is exploring CLM plus eInvoicing/AP Automation to centralize procurement, with no mention of AI agents, Merlin, ANA, or autonomous capabilities in notes.
• Sr. Procurement Manager Melissa is forming a vendor evaluation committee and is already in talks with other vendors, suggesting a traditional features-driven RFP.
• Early Qualified with disco and demo yet to be scheduled and zero AI signal in the current narrative.

The single highest-leverage move is for the deal owner to directly re-engage Melissa (email + LinkedIn + phone) this week to confirm if the evaluation is still active. If no reply within 7 business days, escalate to the deal owner's manager for an executive-to-executive check-in with a senior Jackson Lewis stakeholder (CFO or CIO from account contact list). If still no engagement, disqualify by end of July and stop investing.

MULTI-THREAD IMMEDIATELY UPON RE-ENGAGEMENT: Single-threaded to Melissa (Sr. Manager, Procurement) with no visibility into the evaluation committee, EB, DM, or cross-functional stakeholders (finance, legal, IT) who must approve a CLM + AP automation decision. The 23 Mar next step promised committee formation but we never met them. Upon re-engagement, demand introductions to at least three committee members, map who controls budget (likely the CFO or finance executive), and identify a champion with power and access. A procurement-only thread in a $90k, multi-product, AI-enabled deal is a loss waiting to happen.

RUN COMPETITIVE DISCOVERY BEFORE DEMO: Four competitors (Coupa, GEP, iValua, JAGGAER) are in play but we have zero intelligence on who has demoed, how the buyer ranks them, or what criteria they are using. Positioning Zycus's differentiation (Merlin AI, integrated CLM + Source-to-Pay) requires knowing the gaps the other vendors leave open. Upon re-engagement, before scheduling a demo, run a 30-minute discovery call to surface: (a) which vendors have already presented, (b) how the committee scored them and on what criteria, (c) what they liked and what fell short, and (d) whether budget and timeline are still live. Then tailor the demo to the gaps Zycus uniquely fills.
